description: Directed sector dependency graph for cascading impact propagation. Sources: ENISA NIS Investments Report 2023, CER Directive (EU) 2022/2557 Annex, ENISA Threat Landscape 2024.

energy.rationale: Energy underpins nearly all critical sectors. Hospital generators last 24-72h. Water pumps, transport signalling, data centres all depend on grid power.

digital_infrastructure.rationale: DNS, cloud, CDN, trust services underpin digital operations across all sectors. Payment systems, government portals, telehealth all depend on connectivity.

transport.rationale: Ambulances, medical supply chains, food distribution, mail delivery all depend on transport infrastructure.

drinking_water.rationale: Hospitals and food production require clean water. Contamination or supply failure has direct health consequences.

ict_service_management.rationale: MSPs/MSSPs manage IT for many sectors. A supply chain compromise through an MSP cascades to all managed clients.

banking.rationale: Banking disruption affects financial markets but limited direct cascade to physical infrastructure.

financial_market.rationale: Trading venue/CCP disruption affects banking but limited physical cascade.

health.rationale: Health is primarily a dependency sink, not a source. Disruption affects patients directly but doesn't cascade to other sectors.

public_administration.rationale: Government IT disruption affects citizens but doesn't cascade to other sector operations.

space.rationale: GNSS/satellite timing underpins transport navigation, telecom synchronisation, and energy grid coordination.

food.rationale: Food disruption affects consumers directly. Supply chain issues are sourced from transport/energy dependencies.

chemicals.rationale: Chemical supply disruption affects water treatment and pharmaceutical production.

propagation_rules.direct_dependency: If sector X is impacted at unavailable/sustained and sector Y has direct dependency on X, Y receives implicit degraded impact
propagation_rules.indirect_dependency: If sector X is impacted at sustained and sector Y has indirect dependency on X, Y receives implicit partial impact
propagation_rules.cascading_derivation: Count all impacted sectors (explicit + propagated) for cascading level
